title: Caramelized Vietnamese Pork Belly Bowl
description: A cozy, caramelized pork belly served over fragrant jasmine rice, accented with a sweet‑savory glaze and fresh cilantro, perfect for a low‑FODMAP date night.
instructions: Cook the jasmine rice with water in a rice cooker or saucepan; let it rest covered.
While the rice cooks, combine golden syrup, soy sauce, fish sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, grated ginger, and lime juice in a bowl to make the glaze.
Pat the pork belly cubes dry and sprinkle lightly with salt; then toss them in the glaze and let marinate for 15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat; add the pork and sear until all sides are golden and crisp, about 4‑5 minutes per side.
Pour the remaining glaze over the pork, reduce heat to medium, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the pork is caramelized, about 10 minutes.
Take the skillet off the heat; give the sauce a final stir to ensure it coats the pork evenly.
Plate the cooked jasmine rice in bowls, top with the caramelized pork belly, and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Serve with lime wedges on the side for an extra burst of citrus.
Enjoy a rich, fragrant bowl of Vietnamese comfort food that’s safe for low‑FODMAP diners.
